description.text:
Bumps [electron](https://github.com/electron/electron) from 33.3.1 to 34.0.1.
Release notes

Sourced from electron's releases.

electron v34.0.1

Release Notes for v34.0.1

Fixes

  • Addressed two possible crashes in the File System Access API. #45260 (Also in 35)
  • Fixed an issue where drag-dropping two directories would cause getAsFileSystemHandle to never resolve. #45258 (Also in 33, 35)
  • The avoidClosingConnections option for session.clearData now properly defaults to false when the dataTypes option is not set. #45208 (Also in 33)

electron v34.0.0

Release Notes for v34.0.0

Stack Upgrades

  • Chromium 132.0.6834.83
    • New in 132
    • New in 131
  • Node 20.18.1
    • Node 20.18.1 blog post
  • V8 13.2

Breaking Changes

  • Made menu bar hidden on fullscreen in Windows. #43402

Features

Additions

  • Added APIs to manage shared dictionaries for compression efficiency using Brotli or ZStandard. The new APIs are session.getSharedDictionaryUsageInfo(), session.getSharedDictionaryInfo(options), session.clearSharedDictionaryCache(), and session.clearSharedDictionaryCacheForIsolationKey(options). #44950 (Also in 33)
  • Added WebFrameMain.collectJavaScriptCallStack() for accessing the JavaScript call stack of unresponsive renderers. #44938 (Also in 33)
  • Added WebFrameMain.detached for frames in an unloading state.
    • Added WebFrameMain.isDestroyed() to determine if a frame has been destroyed.
    • Fixed webFrameMain.fromId(processId, frameId) returning a WebFrameMain instance which doesn't match the given parameters when the frame is unloading. #43473 (Also in 33)
  • Added error event in utility process to support diagnostic reports on V8 fatal errors. #43774 (Also in 33)
  • Added support for the macOS system picker in desktopCapturer and setDisplayMediaRequestHandler. #43581 (Also in 32, 33)
  • Feat: GPU accelerated shared texture offscreen rendering. #42953 (Also in 33)

Improvements

  • Performance improvements when processing microtasks. #44471 (Also in 32, 33)

Fixes

  • Fixed file chooser dialogs for flaptak applications. #44681
  • Fixed issue where 'contextmenu' event is emitted twice on macOS. #44997

Also in earlier versions...

  • Fixed BrowserView auto resize issue. #43463 (Also in 31, 32, 33)
  • Fixed MenuItem.click handler not setting focusedWindow parameter when focused window is a BaseWindow. #43404 (Also in 31, 32, 33)
  • Fixed chrome.i18n extension API being unavailable in service workers. #45036 (Also in 31, 32, 33)
  • Fixed trace-startup not working on macOS. #44275 (Also in 32, 33)
  • Fixed a build failure when the enable_plugins build flag is false. #44003 (Also in 33)

... (truncated)

Commits
  • 4a1c029 refactor: in StopTracing(), use string literals instead of `optional<strin...
  • a1816f3 docs: add DocCardList component for index doc (#45295)
  • db84f39 refactor: simplify ParseUserScript() (#45289)
  • 7ecb8ad chore: align clipboard blink::web_pref::WebPreferences with upstream (#45279)
  • 973c344 fix: getAsFileSystemHandle failure when drag-dropping two directories (#45258)
  • 01c5db0 build: fix clang-format duplicate message (#45264)
  • 2c22507 fix: two possible FSA crashes (#45260)
  • 41c1161 fix: session.clearData avoidClosingConnections default to false (#45208)
  • b36aaf3 docs: remove quickstart (#45227)
  • 5728e6d docs: Why Electron? (#45211)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot show ignore conditions` will show all of the ignore conditions of the specified dependency - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself)

### Description

The `View stats > More statistics … > Graph` view is extremely useful when using Zettlr as a Zettelkasten tool and, more generally, for assembling writing projects. It allows the user to easily identify a through-line between connected documents, which is a great first step toward needle-and-threading them into a larger writing project.

However, the graph view, as currently constituted, does not account at all for how the user has organized the documents it is graphing. For example, if I have a folder for notes dedicated to a particular writing project, I may well wish to see connections just between those items and not between every document across every folder I'm managing in Zettlr.

If, over time, I move documents between a workspace folder for reading notes, another for unpublished scraps of writing, another for notes that have already been incorporated into publications, and still others for various writing projects, I'm going to have a large tangle of connections between documents in different workspace folders. I think an interface option to restrict the graph view by workspace would allow the user to much more easily generate a useful display.

### Proposed Changes

When using the `Global search` feature, there's a handy `Restrict to directory` dropdown menu that allows the user to limit their results to the workspace they're interested in. I think implementing this could be as straightforward as including the same dropdown among the graph view controls.

### Caveats

I can imagine other future enhancements to graph view, such as generating a graph view of the links between notes returned in search results or — if multiple selection ever gets implemented in the file manager — between items in a batch of notes the user has selected manually. Maybe, from a design perspective, it's worth thinking about the present request in the context of a broader array of future graph-view features. On the other hand, I think the present request is comparatively straightforward to implement, whereas these other ideas are much more speculative and labor-intensive.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Nightly

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Dismissing Tabs Can Create a Visual Glitch in the File Manager's Thin View

description.text:
### Description

When you have a workspace open using the file manager's `Thin` view and have opened multiple tabs from the documents in that workspace, closing these tabs can bring on a visual glitch in the file manager, particularly when the files are at points in the file list far enough apart to require scrolling. I realize that all this is hard to describe, so I've made a video. (Pardon and ignore the long silence at the end where I hunt for the tray icon to stop the recording.)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d5d859ce-49dd-4bc0-8e3a-3eecb69457ff

### Reproducing

1. Set the file manager to `Thin` view in preferences.
2. Open a workspace with enough files that you must scroll to see the bottom of the list.
3. Open a file from near the top of the list in one tab.
4. Open a file from near the bottom of the list in another.
5. Close the second file.

### Zettlr version

3.4.1 Nightly from January 13, 2025

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

I use the appimage.

summary: Solution for inadvertent spellchecking of Chinese characters

description.text:
### Description

There has been some discussion on this problem, but I will reiterate.

When using the spellchecker for, say, English but also typing Chinese, i.e. bilingual use with a character-based language, the spellchecker will mark every character of the latter language as containing errors (red underline).

As for Chinese, as stated here in this comment https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/303#issuecomment-922506843, and I have had similar results with my own search, there are no dictionaries available for Chinese. You might even argue that it is impractical to implement such a dictionary at all. Please point me to one, if you know something different.

The issue has been discussed and transferred into https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/2489, which is essentially a completely different feature request, that has been closed as not planned due to different reasons. The original problem from issue #303 however still persists.

I have searched documentation, as well as previous issues, and have found no solution or workaround. No open issue addresses this point.

### Proposed Changes

Since there is no practical way to get Chinese spellchecking to work with the current system: **ignore Chinese characters entirely for spellchecking**. Do not mark them incorrect at all, and don't attempt to check them.

As a mitigation to caveats, this feature should possibly be a toggle.

(People suggested alternatives, like allowing for a custom regex filter, of which characters to ignore, which could also solve the problem, but in my eyes the solution above would be better, since there is no use for using the current spellchecker on Chinese characters.)

### Caveats

There may be other languages than Chinese that are character-based, that also lack or cannot reasonably have support via a dictionary the current spellchecker system.

Japanese does have spellchecker dictionaries that I think, will contain Chinese characters as well and should be checked. To mitigate this, ignoring Chinese character (Hanzi/Kanji) could be a toggle.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Stable (most recent version)

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Fedora 41

### Additional Information

Thanks for developing Zettlr. It's very useful for my work and I appreciate the time and effort you put into it!

### Description

The files in the `~/.config/Zettlr/fsal/cache/` folder contain a lot of metadata from the notes. This is very bad because if someone decides to use an external tool for encryption (e.g. VeraCrypt or Cryptomator) that folder leaks metadata.

That folder should be purged automatically when the app closes, or at least a setting should be provided to enable the automatic purge at closure. (The button to manually purge it is not enough).

This is not a small issue as this kind of software is meant to contain very sensitive data.

### Reproducing

- Purge the `~/.config/Zettlr/fsal/cache/` folder
- Launch the app
- Close the app
- The folder is still populated

### Zettlr version

3.4.1

### Installation Method

Flathub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)
